items.summary: Aviation fuel regulations in India have been updated by the Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas. The Aviation Turbine Fuel (Regulation of Marketing) Amendment Order, 2026, refines the fuel's definition to include synthetic hydrocarbon blends and removes outdated company references. Crucially, search and seizure powers now align with the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita, 2023, enhancing enforcement.

Public infrastructure projects could be deferred to manage expenditure.

items.title: Amazon signs $30 million deal to buy carbon credits from Indian rice farmers

items.title: India seafood exports to US fall amid tariffs
items.summary: India’s seafood exports to the US fell 19.8% in volume and 14.5% in value in FY26, hit by reciprocal tariffs, the commerce ministry said. However, overall exports hit a record $8.28 billion. Growth in China, the EU and Southeast Asia helped offset the US decline.

items.title: India, Taiwan get time till October to settle ICT tariff dispute

Both nations are actively engaged in mutual discussions to resolve the dispute. This marks the ninth such delay, as previous requests were also granted.

These rules aim to protect customers and ensure secure electronic transactions across borders.

Major exporting nations are intensifying their biodiesel initiatives, stripping away palm oil for local energy applications. This trend, paired with the potential El Nino effects on Indian rainfall patterns and fluctuating global political tensions, is likely to limit the availability of exportable palm oil.
